Tests if the type of `null` is `'object'`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion statement that checks the type of the `null` value in JavaScript. It verifies that the type of `null` is equal to the string `'object'`. This is a known behavior in JavaScript where `null` is considered an object type, despite being a primitive value. The code serves as a test or validation to ensure that this specific behavior of the JavaScript language is consistent and expected.
